rule_name: Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment

text: when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%patch%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%apps%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%deployments%' AQL filter query

_$.description: Converted from SIGMA Rule.  Original Author: Leo Tsaousis (@laripping) SIGMA Title: Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment SIGMA Description: Detects attempts to inject a sidecar container into a running deployment. A sidecar container is an additional container within a pod, that resides alongside the main container. One way to add containers to running resources like Deployments/DeamonSets/StatefulSets, is via a "kubectl patch" operation. By injecting a new container within a legitimate pod, an attacker can run their code and hide their activity, instead of running their own separated pod in the cluster.   False positives: - Unknown  This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
_$.name: Potential Sidecar Injection Into Running Deployment
